sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this sports title

special_mechanics.name: Event-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: Game features 15 distinct sporting events with unique movement mechanics per event (fencing, triple jump, freestyle swimming, high dive, clay pigeon shooting, hammer throw, taekwondo, pole vault, canoeing, archery, hurdles, horizontal bar, hang gliding, arm wrestling, gun firing)

special_mechanics.name: NES Zapper Support
special_mechanics.description: Gun Firing bonus event supports optional NES Zapper controller in addition to standard controller input

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative. Track & Field II is a sports compilation game where movement mechanics vary significantly by event type. No precise physics parameters are documented in available sources. Game focused on arcade-style event simulation rather than platform movement mechanics. Sources provide game mode descriptions, event lists, and regional/revisional differences, but contain no technical physics data.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Track & Field II (NES)
